title: Introducing Legend of Sanctamonium - 20 year daily YouTube Musical

enrichment.summary: Kyle introduces 'Legend of Sanctamonium,' a 20-year daily YouTube musical/TV show he plans to produce from age 42 to 62, set in a fantasy world superimposed on Boulder, CO that mirrors ancient Rome exactly — town for town, battle for battle, tribe for tribe — on a 50:1 time compression ratio. The show combines costumed court speeches, bicycle rides to reenact Roman military campaigns, musical elements, and Google Earth mapping, with the explicit educational goal that fans who follow the show will learn the full history of Rome. He also sketches out 'Bikeversy,' a related framework for graded bicycle achievement tying physical performance to warrior ranks in Sanctamonium.
enrichment.speaker: kyle
topics: pyramid symbolism and capital city design

stances.position: a genuinely new and superior genre of television because it is the most educational
bicycles deserve more trail rights than horses — they cause less damage, are cheaper, and more democratic
horses are a rich man's sport and unfairly privileged; bicycles should replace them as the primary trail vehicle
a cycling lifestyle keeps people physically and mentally strong, which prevents political complacency and cultural decay
learning history through cycling teaches appreciation for local economies and should inspire people to keep production and commerce close to home
motorbikes ruin the experiential and educational value of trail riding and would make trails dangerous and overcrowded
fantasy framing allows more creativity while still conveying the same lessons as real history, and parable helps people grasp concepts across cultures
